Resident Resource Guide

Best Practices

Simple guidelines to protect your home, your deposit, and your neighbors — so everyone thrives.

🚫
Garbage Disposal Do's & Don'ts

✓ Do Put In

  • Small soft food scraps
  • Citrus rinds (freshens drain)
  • Cooked meat (small pieces)
  • Ice cubes (cleans blades)
  • Soft vegetable peels

✗ Never Put In

  • Bones or fruit pits
  • Grease, fat, or oils
  • Pasta, rice, or bread
  • Coffee grounds
  • Fibrous veggies (celery, husks)
  • Paper, plastic, or packaging
⚠️ Security Deposit Note Disposal damage from misuse is considered tenant negligence under California leases. Repairs ($150–$400+) may be deducted from your deposit.
🌬️
HVAC Filter Maintenance
1

Locate your filter. It's usually in the return air vent (a large vent on the wall or ceiling) or in the air handler unit itself.

2

Change every 1–3 months. Homes with pets or allergy sufferers should change monthly. Standard homes every 60–90 days.

3

Buy the right size. The filter dimensions are printed on the side of the existing filter (e.g. 16×20×1).

4

Note the airflow arrow. When inserting, the arrow on the filter should point toward the air handler, not toward you.

5

Notify us if the system seems off. Strange noises, weak airflow, or no heat/cool — submit a maintenance request right away.

💡 Why it matters A clogged filter makes the HVAC work harder, raises your utility bill, and can cause expensive equipment damage. This is one of the easiest ways to protect your home.
🔧
Reporting Maintenance Issues
1

Report promptly. Small issues become expensive repairs when ignored. A dripping faucet can waste thousands of gallons and cause water damage.

2

Email us at info@housemynt.com. Describe the issue, the location in the unit, and when it started.

3

Include a photo or video when possible — this speeds up our response significantly.

4

For emergencies (flooding, gas smell, no heat in winter, broken entry lock) — call 323.304.1564 immediately.

5

Allow reasonable access. Once a repair is scheduled, please be available or leave a key to allow our vendor entry during the agreed window.

📋
Move-In / Move-Out Checklist

Move-In — Day One

Document existing damage with dated photos
Test all light switches and outlets
Run all faucets and check for leaks
Test smoke and CO detectors
Check all window locks and door locks
Email condition notes to House Mynt within 48 hrs

Move-Out — Before Keys

Deep clean all appliances inside & out
Clean bathrooms, toilets, grout lines
Patch nail holes, touch up paint if needed
Remove all trash and personal belongings
Return all keys, fobs, and garage remotes
Leave unit in same condition as move-in
📸 Pro tip Take a full video walkthrough on move-in day and again before you hand in keys. This protects you and ensures a smooth deposit return.
🐜
Pest Prevention
Keep food sealed in containers Empty trash regularly Don't leave pet food out overnight Fix drips — pests seek water Seal gaps around pipes & vents Keep counters & stovetop clean Report signs immediately Don't store cardboard boxes long-term
🚨 Report it fast If you see signs of rodents, cockroaches, bed bugs, or other pests — email us immediately at info@housemynt.com. Early reporting prevents infestations from spreading and protects your neighbors.
🛡️
Renter's Insurance Importance

What It Covers

  • Your personal belongings (theft, fire, water damage)
  • Temporary housing if unit becomes uninhabitable
  • Personal liability if someone is injured in your unit
  • Medical payments to others injured on the property

What It Does NOT Cover

  • The building structure (owner's responsibility)
  • Floods (requires separate flood policy)
  • Earthquakes (requires separate policy)
  • Roommate's belongings without their own policy
💰 Cost & Requirement Renter's insurance typically costs $10–$20/month — less than a dinner out. House Mynt strongly recommends all tenants maintain an active policy. It protects you, not the building.
🔋
Smoke & Carbon Monoxide Detectors
1

Test monthly. Press and hold the test button until it beeps. If no beep, replace the battery immediately.

2

Replace batteries annually — or when the unit chirps every 30–60 seconds (low battery warning).

3

Never remove or disable detectors. This is a lease violation and a California fire code violation.

4

CO alarm? Leave immediately, leave the door open, call 911 from outside. Do not re-enter until cleared by fire department.

5

Report malfunctions. If a detector is beeping abnormally or appears damaged, email us right away — we'll replace it.

⚖️ California Law California requires carbon monoxide detectors in all residential units. Tenants are responsible for notifying management of non-functioning detectors.
🌙
Quiet Hours — 10 PM to 7 AM
10 PM – 7 AM

Quiet hours are in effect every night. During this window, please keep music, TV, gatherings, and foot traffic at a level that does not disturb neighboring units. This applies seven days a week, including weekends and holidays.

Keep music & TV at low volume No loud gatherings or parties Minimize foot traffic & stomping No power tools or drilling Dogs should not bark continuously Courtesy to neighbors always
📢 Noise Complaints Repeated violations of quiet hours are a lease violation and may result in a formal notice or lease termination. If a neighbor is disrupting you, email info@housemynt.com and we'll address it directly.

Questions? We're here for you.
